title: Lec-57: Need of IPv6 Protocol | Why IPv6 is Required

segments.text: - Dear students, welcome to Gate Smashers
- Today in this video I am going to explain why there is need of IPv6 protocol
- Guys, at maximum places you must have read about IPv4
- either you talk about competitive exams for college, university exams, still there
- maximum questions are asked about IPv4 but
- now the IPv6 protocol has become very popular
- and you should know the detail about this
- although I have already made a video about header of IPv6
- that what are the fields in it's header and what are the purpose of that fields but
- often in competitive exams and interviews they ask that what is the need of IPv6
- when IPv4 is already working very nicely then what is the need of IPv6
- what are the reasons that we're shifting to IPv6? So I want to clear these point in this video
- so guys quickly like the video, subscribe to the channel
- if you haven't done yet and even if you've done then you can get it subscribed by other devices
- subscribers are really important. Ok so let's start, first point is what we call IPv6
- we call it as IPng protocol also, as IPng protocol
- it means IP next generation. Okay? So whenever we talk about network layer protocol, then IP protocol comes in that
- and in IP, IPv4 and IPv6 are two versions
- and IPv6 is the latest version, we call it as next generation also, because it is made
- the purpose of making it is that you will not need next version for next 50-60 years and you can work easily on it
- and actually what is happening is that, till now we are trying to connect our laptops, mobiles to the internet, we were connecting these
- but since the concept of iot came, since the concept of internet of things came, it says that the regular devices of our house
- AC, washing machine, fridge, or any other thing, means you are connecting the laptop and mobile phones
- other than that you are also connecting all these devices to the internet
- means you are connecting home appliances, that's why you might have heard smart homes, smart watches. All these are connected through internet
- so if this much devices are being connected then obviously I will need high no. of IP addresses
- okay? Because if there will be IP address then only it will connect to the internet
- so that's why this is the first reason why we are shifting to IPv6, why we are preferring IPv6
- because there is a limitation in the IPv4 addresses. So the addresses that were in the IPv4
- in that you might have heard that addresses of 32 bit, so address of 32 bit means
- you can generate IP addresses till 2³²
- and see this is the value of 2³², mean approximately you can say 4.3 billion values
- this value for sufficient till now, but since the concept of iot came
- since then this value became less
- in that you must have heard this also, when classful addressing was there, we reserved class D class E for different purposes
- although through classless addressing or network address translation, the concept of NAT
- we generated so much addresses through that also, still our purpose was solved
- but since the concept of iot came, so much devices have came, that is why number of IP addresses I've was limited there
- that is why we are using IPv6 now because as you can see here, it is difficult to even judge the value
- the value is in quintillions. This is a very big value, you can generate this much number of addresses
- so you can say, this number is sufficient for upcoming 50-60 years, so you can say this is the first and very big reason but
- this is not the only reason, there are many more reasons, like real time data transmission
- whenever we talk about real time data transmission, either you talk about live streaming, we have to transmit audio video instantly
- immediately, means, sender send it and receiver should receive it immediately, like we stream live
- like you watch matches, if we talk about cricket matches, it isn't like he threw the ball and you will get to see it after 10 minutes, no
- in live stream, there might be a slight delay, but still it should be matched
- you can say that in IPv4 we don't have these type of field or support system
- that supported this but in IPv6 we have support system for real time data transmission
- that is why, this is also one of advantage. Then, authentication
- if we talk about authentication, authentication means the message should be authenticated
- means, if I sent you a message, and you received that message, you should be confirm that this message was sent by me only
- means, that message was sent through me only, no one else is sending message as varun sir
- so for this, authenticate that message, like we used hashing etc by taking out message digest
- and then we match that hash value and if it matches then it is ok
- means this concept comes under authentication and it supports it means IPv6 supports it
- then, similarly there is the concept of the encryption. Earlier, application layer
- user said that I want encryption, then the protocols of application layer
- send the data after encrypting it. There was no such system in network layer in IPv4
- there was no system of encryption, that convert the plain text into cypher text
- but in IPv6, if the application layer, the above layer
- is a sending encrypted data, then it's good. And if it is not doing so then this thing is enabled at the network layer, means, IPv6
- can also encrypt the message. Security is also there
- that is a major or crucial point in today's world
- because so much devices are being connected, many people are related to e-commerce, so obviously security is a major concern
- so this is also a major advantage of this. Then, fast processing at the routers, means
- what happened? There are so many fields in IPv4, means, if you see their header
- there was a variable header from 20 to 60 byte. Means, you've to judge and calculate so many fields on router
- either you have to check the length of the header, you had to calculate that also
- other than that, checksum, there were so many values but here in IPv6 it fixed the base header of 40 byte
- it removed to some things from there so that there won't be extra processing, so the router doesn't take extra time
- and it could process quickly, okay? Although, it kept other things in the extension header
- but they fixed the base header, so because of being light or you can say due to deleting somethings
- routers can process the packets of IPv6 very quickly
- so that's why fast processing is also. This is also the advantage
- if you are asked from these points, or in case you are asked what is the advantage of IPv6
- why is IPv6 different from IPv4? What are its benefits? Then you can mention these points
- you won't be asked anything more than this. Thank you
